raw_data.details: This scheme is open to Indian citizens under the age of 45 who hold a Ph. D. and have demonstrated research ability through earlier publications. All candidates must be affiliated with a Ministry of Education or UGC-recognized public-funded Indian university, and a senior faculty member (not less than an Associate Professor) from the same institution must supervise their study. The fellowship offers a ₹31,000 monthly grant and ₹20,000 annual contingency grant for a period of two years. The applications for the fellowship are invited through advertising on the ICSSR website and social media platforms. The scheme covers a wide range of disciplines under the discipline of social sciences, with 26 major areas including priority research topics. In addition to conducting full-time research, fellows are expected to provide progress updates on a regular basis and publish at least two research publications in reputable journals. The final submission includes published articles, audited financial data, summaries, and a book-length report. Given India's social science ecosystem, it seeks to promote research excellence, evidence-based research, and scholarship that is also relevant to policymakers.
raw_data.benefits: 1) Monthly Fellowship amount @ ₹31,000 per month2) Contingency Grant @ ₹20,000 per annum3) API points as per the UGC Guidelines in force at the point of time4) Institutional overhead (7. 5%) - supports the hosting institution.
raw_data.eligibility: 1) The applicant must be an Indian citizen. 2) Must be under 45 years of age (5-year relaxation for SC/ST/PwD). 3) Must possess a Ph. D. in a social science discipline. 4) Must have demonstrable research competence via publications. 5) Must affiliate with a public-funded Indian institution approved for Ph. D. 6) Must have a supervisor who is a serving faculty (Associate Professor or above). 7) Applicant must not be a defaulter of any ICSSR grant/project/fellowship.
raw_data.exclusions: 1) Those who are not fulfilling the edibility criteria.
raw_data.application_process:
Online
Step 01: Visit ICSSR website - [www.icssr.org](https://icssr.org) - and navigate to Fellowships section.
Step 02: Click on Post-Doctoral Fellowship Scheme.
Step 03: Register/Login to the ICSSR portal.
Step 04: Fill the online application form.
Step 05: Upload documents and submit.
raw_data.documents_required: The list of documents required will be given in the Application Form when the Call is announced.

To know more please visit Check EligibilityMinistry of EducationICSSR Post-Doctoral FellowshipICSSR FellowshipsPost-Doctoral FellowshipResearch PromotionSocial Science ResearchSocial ScientistsDetailsThis scheme is open to Indian citizens under the age of 45 who hold a Ph. D. and have demonstrated research ability through earlier publications. All candidates must be affiliated with a Ministry of Education or UGC-recognized public-funded Indian university, and a senior faculty member (not less than an Associate Professor) from the same institution must supervise their study. The fellowship offers a ₹31,000 monthly grant and ₹20,000 annual contingency grant for a period of two years. The applications for the fellowship are invited through advertising on the ICSSR website and social media platforms. The scheme covers a wide range of disciplines under the discipline of social sciences, with 26 major areas including priority research topics. In addition to conducting full-time research, fellows are expected to provide progress updates on a regular basis and publish at least two research publications in reputable journals. The final submission includes published articles, audited financial data, summaries, and a book-length report. Given India's social science ecosystem, it seeks to promote research excellence, evidence-based research, and scholarship that is also relevant to policymakers. Benefits1) Monthly Fellowship amount @ ₹31,000 per month2) Contingency Grant @ ₹20,000 per annum3) API points as per the UGC Guidelines in force at the point of time4) Institutional overhead (7. 5%) - supports the hosting institution. Eligibility1) The applicant must be an Indian citizen. 2) Must be under 45 years of age (5-year relaxation for SC/ST/PwD). 3) Must possess a Ph. D. in a social science discipline. 4) Must have demonstrable research competence via publications. 5) Must affiliate with a public-funded Indian institution approved for Ph. D. 6) Must have a supervisor who is a serving faculty (Associate Professor or above). 7) Applicant must not be a defaulter of any ICSSR grant/project/fellowship. Exclusions1) Those who are not fulfilling the edibility criteria. Application ProcessOnlineStep 01: Visit ICSSR website - [www.icssr.org](https://icssr.org) - and navigate to Fellowships section. Step 02: Click on Post-Doctoral Fellowship Scheme. Step 03: Register/Login to the ICSSR portal. Step 04: Fill the online application form. Step 05: Upload documents and submit. Documents RequiredThe list of documents required will be given in the Application Form when the Call is announced. Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the monthly fellowship amount? ₹31,000 per month. What is the contingency grant used for? Research-related expenses like books, fieldwork, and stationery. Do I need to have publications? Yes, fellows are expected to publish at least two peer-reviewed papers. How is the fellowship amount disbursed? The amount is disbursed in instalments through the affiliating institution upon submission of the required reports and bills. Can I apply if I am over 45? Only if you qualify for SC/ST/PwD categories - age relaxation (up to 5 years). Is it mandatory to have a Ph. D. before applying? Yes, Ph. D. is required at the time of application. Can the research topic be the same as my Ph. D. thesis? No, it must be an original and distinct study. Can I apply if I am affiliated with a private university? No, only public-funded institutions are eligible. Can I have any other job or project during the fellowship? No, full-time research is mandatory. Where can I apply for the fellowship? Through the ICSSR website during the open call. What language can I use for the proposal? English or Hindi (Unicode 8/UTF-8). Can I submit multiple applications under this scheme? No, only one application is allowed per call. What documents are required after selection? All the documents mentioned in the call or as communicated via phone or email. Sources And References[ICSSR Post-Doctoral Fellowship](https://icssr.org/post-doctoral-fellowship)OkWas this helpful?

faqs.question: What is the monthly fellowship amount?
What is the contingency grant used for?
faqs.answer: Research-related expenses like books, fieldwork, and stationery.

faqs.question: Do I need to have publications?
faqs.answer: Yes, fellows are expected to publish at least two peer-reviewed papers.

faqs.question: How is the fellowship amount disbursed?
faqs.answer: The amount is disbursed in instalments through the affiliating institution upon submission of the required reports and bills.

faqs.question: Can I apply if I am over 45?
faqs.answer: Only if you qualify for SC/ST/PwD categories - age relaxation (up to 5 years).

faqs.question: Is it mandatory to have a Ph. D. before applying?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ph. D. is required at the time of application.

faqs.question: Can the research topic be the same as my Ph. D. thesis?
faqs.answer: No, it must be an original and distinct study.

faqs.question: Can I apply if I am affiliated with a private university?
faqs.answer: No, only public-funded institutions are eligible.

faqs.question: Can I have any other job or project during the fellowship?
Where can I apply for the fellowship?
faqs.answer: Through the ICSSR website during the open call.

faqs.question: What language can I use for the proposal?
Can I submit multiple applications under this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, only one application is allowed per call.

faqs.question: What documents are required after selection?
faqs.answer: All the documents mentioned in the call or as communicated via phone or email.
